revision.text:
{{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyogo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} and {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of {{ill|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)|ja|真如寺}} in {{ill|Nose, Osaka|ja|能勢町}}, and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class {{ill|Triangulation station|ja|三角点}} is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for {{ill|Japanese beech|ja|ブナ}} at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|川西市}}, and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and {{ill|Izumikatsuragi Mountain|ja|和泉葛城山}} are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏}}, descended from {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|ja|源満仲}}, began venerating {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}. In the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Nissei, the 21st abbot of {{ill|Kuon-ji|ja|久遠寺}}, carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)}}. * From {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From {{ill|Kameoka Station|ja|亀岡駅}} (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From {{ill|Myōken-guchi Station|ja|妙見口駅}} (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier {{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyogo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} and {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of {{ill|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)|ja|真如寺}} in {{ill|Nose, Osaka|ja|能勢町}}, and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class {{ill|Triangulation station|ja|三角点}} is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for {{ill|Japanese beech|ja|ブナ}} at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|川西市}}, and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and {{ill|Izumikatsuragi Mountain|ja|和泉葛城山}} are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏}}, descended from {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|ja|源満仲}}, began venerating {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}. In the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Nissei, the 21st abbot of {{ill|Kuon-ji|ja|久遠寺}}, carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)}}. * From {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From {{ill|Kameoka Station|ja|亀岡駅}} (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From {{ill|Myōken-guchi Station|ja|妙見口駅}} (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧}} * revision.text:
{{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyogo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} and {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of {{ill|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)|ja|真如寺}} in {{ill|Nose, Osaka|ja|能勢町}}, and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class {{ill|Triangulation station|ja|三角点}} is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for {{ill|Japanese beech|ja|ブナ}} at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|川西市}}, and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and {{ill|Izumikatsuragi Mountain|ja|和泉葛城山}} are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏}}, descended from {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|ja|源満仲}}, began venerating {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}. In the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Nissei, the 21st abbot of {{ill|Kuon-ji|ja|久遠寺}}, carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)}}. * From {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From {{ill|Kameoka Station|ja|亀岡駅}} (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From {{ill|Myōken-guchi Station|ja|妙見口駅}} (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also! == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier revision.text:
{{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyogo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} and {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of {{ill|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)|ja|真如寺}} in {{ill|Nose, Osaka|ja|能勢町}}, and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class {{ill|Triangulation station|ja|三角点}} is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for {{ill|Japanese beech|ja|ブナ}} at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|川西市}}, and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and {{ill|Izumikatsuragi Mountain|ja|和泉葛城山}} are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏}}, descended from {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|ja|源満仲}}, began venerating {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}. In the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Nissei, the 21st abbot of {{ill|Kuon-ji|ja|久遠寺}}, carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)}}. * From {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From {{ill|Kameoka Station|ja|亀岡駅}} (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From {{ill|Myōken-guchi Station|ja|妙見口駅}} (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of {{ill|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)|ja|真如寺}} in {{ill|Nose, Osaka|ja|能勢町}}, and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class {{ill|Triangulation station|ja|三角点}} is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for {{ill|Japanese beech|ja|ブナ}} at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|川西市}}, and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and {{ill|Izumikatsuragi Mountain|ja|和泉葛城山}} are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏}}, descended from {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|ja|源満仲}}, began venerating {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}. In the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Nissei, the 21st abbot of {{ill|Kuon-ji|ja|久遠寺}}, carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)}}. * From {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From {{ill|Kameoka Station|ja|亀岡駅}} (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From {{ill|Myōken-guchi Station|ja|妙見口駅}} (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] {{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyogo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} and {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of {{ill|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)|ja|真如寺}} in {{ill|Nose, Osaka|ja|能勢町}}, and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class {{ill|Triangulation station|ja|三角点}} is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for {{ill|Japanese beech|ja|ブナ}} at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|川西市}}, and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and {{ill|Izumikatsuragi Mountain|ja|和泉葛城山}} are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏}}, descended from {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|ja|源満仲}}, began venerating {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}. In the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Nissei, the 21st abbot of {{ill|Kuon-ji|ja|久遠寺}}, carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)}}. * From {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From {{ill|Kameoka Station|ja|亀岡駅}} (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From {{ill|Myōken-guchi Station|ja|妙見口駅}} (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} {{Hokusetsu Mountains Transport and Attractions}} {{Kansai 100 Famous Mountains}} {{Osaka 50 Mountains}} {{Osaka Green 100 Selection}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Tamba Highlands]] [[Category:Mountains of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Hyōgo]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Osaka]] [[Category:Kawanishi, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Toyono, Osaka]] [[Category:Nose, Osaka]] [[Category:Prefectural Borders]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[ja:妙見山_(大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[en:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)}}

revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
{{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市|12=draft}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡|12=draft}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyogo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}} and {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=draft}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|12=draft}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of {{ill|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)|ja|真如寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nose, Osaka|ja|能勢町|12=draft}}, and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class {{ill|Triangulation station|ja|三角点|12=draft}} is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for {{ill|Japanese beech|ja|ブナ|12=draft}} at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|川西市|12=draft}}, and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and {{ill|Izumikatsuragi Mountain|ja|和泉葛城山|12=draft}} are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|12=draft}}, descended from {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|ja|源満仲|12=draft}}, began venerating {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|12=draft}}. In the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, Nissei, the 21st abbot of {{ill|Kuon-ji|ja|久遠寺|12=draft}}, carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|12=draft}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|12=draft}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|12=draft}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|12=draft}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|12=draft}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|12=draft}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=draft}}. * From {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|12=draft}} (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From {{ill|Kameoka Station|ja|亀岡駅|12=draft}} (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From {{ill|Myōken-guchi Station|ja|妙見口駅|12=draft}} (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} {{Hokusetsu Mountains Transport and Attractions}} {{Kansai 100 Famous Mountains}} {{Osaka 50 Mountains}} {{Osaka Green 100 Selection}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Tamba Highlands]] [[Category:Mountains of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Hyōgo]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Osaka]] [[Category:Kawanishi, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Toyono, Osaka]] [[Category:Nose, Osaka]] [[Category:Prefectural Borders]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[ja:妙見山_(大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[en:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)}}

{{Redirect|能勢妙見山|山頂付近にある[[霊場]]|能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|中腹にある[[寺院]]|本瀧寺}} {{Infobox 山 |名称=妙見山 |画像=[[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] |標高=660.1 |山系 = [[北摂山系]] |座標={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|}} |所在地=兵庫県川西市、
大阪府豊能郡豊能町・能勢町 |地図={{location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyogo Prefecture|width=300|relief=1}}{{日本の位置情報|34|55|44|135|27|59|妙見山|nocoord=1}} |OSMズーム = 10 }} '''妙見山'''（みょうけんさん、みょうけんざん）は、[[兵庫県]]、[[大阪府]]にまたがる[[山]]。[[北摂山系]]に属する。 == 概要 == 山体が信仰の対象であり、山頂近くには[[日蓮宗]]の関西地区における重要寺院である''[[能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|能勢妙見堂]] ''がある。能勢妙見堂は正式名称を無漏山真如寺境外仏堂（けいがいぶつどう）能勢妙見山と称し、能勢町地黄にある[[真如寺 (大阪府能勢町)|真如寺]]の飛び地境内であるが、本寺の真如寺よりもはるかに多くの参詣者を集めている。 山頂には4等[[三角点]]が設置されている。三角点の所在地は[[大阪府]][[豊能郡]][[豊能町]]で、記念碑の陰の目立たない場所に標識が設置されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google ストリートビュー 妙見山三角点 |accessdate=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google ストリートビュー]] }}。 当山は西南日本の標高600メートル付近での[[ブナ]]の自生の南限とされ、これまでは大阪府側のみに分布すると考えられてきたが、[[兵庫県]][[川西市]]で8本のブナが発見され（この個体群は2013年に兵庫県のレッドリストに追加された）http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdf、さらに山頂付近のブナ林（9.5[[ヘクタール]]）の一部が兵庫県側に入っていることも確認されたhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/。大阪府下のブナ自生地は妙見山と[[和泉葛城山]]のみであり、いずれも天然記念物に指定されている。 == 歴史 == === 山頂 === 山頂には[[行基]]の建立を伝える為楽山大空寺があった。鎌倉時代に入ると[[源満仲]]を祖とする[[能勢氏]]が領主となりその地に[[妙見菩薩]]を祀ったとされる。その後、江戸時代初期に当時の領主能勢頼次の帰依を受けた[[日乾]]（後の[[日蓮宗]]総本山である身延山[[久遠寺]]二十一世）の手によって新たな妙見菩薩像が彫られ、大空寺趾に建立した仏堂に祀ったのが現在の'''[[能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|能勢妙見堂]]'''である。 山頂の境内地に残るブナ林は一万年以上前から残る自然林である。そのことより、[[行基]]による大空寺建立以前から山頂付近には何らかの信仰があったことがうかがえる。 === 中腹の滝 === 北側中腹には能勢の本滝があり、江戸時代より滝に打たれる者が増えた。江戸時代中期に刊行された[[摂津名所図会]]には能勢の本滝ではなく妙見滝とあり、能勢妙見堂の参詣者が打たれた滝であった。当時、滝の横には常富堂があったとされる。[[1834年]]に編纂された能勢東郷志においても参詣者が行をする妙見山滝と記述されており、滝が境内地の一部として栄えていたことがうかがえる。 しかし、妙見山は[[旗本]]である[[能勢氏]]の[[知行地]]であったため、明治政府の[[廃藩置県]]や[[版籍奉還]]の影響を少なからず受け、その後[[天台宗]]系[[修験道]]の僧侶である野間日照が、妙見瀧の地を野間中村より取得し居を構えた。[[1935年]]には野間日照により滝の横にあった常富堂を改築する形で[[本瀧寺]]（現在の[[妙見宗]]総本山）を建立した。現在、能勢の本滝までの山道に多くの石仏が祀られているのを見ることができるが、これは能勢の本滝が行場であった時代の名残である。 他に東側中腹に清滝、南西側中腹に新滝（雄滝）と呼ばれる滝があり、清滝の側には[[妙瀧寺]]がある。また新滝は白瀧神社が鎮座している。 == 交通 == 山頂（能勢妙見堂本堂）までは以下のアクセスがある。詳しくは[[能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|能勢妙見山（日蓮宗）]]を参照。 * 阪急宝塚線[[池田駅 (大阪府)|池田駅]]から阪急バス東能勢線のりかえ「妙見口」停留所（豊能町野間口、国道423号線沿い）から徒歩4km。 ** 阪急バス東能勢線は「中止々呂美」停留所で、北大阪急行線[[千里中央駅]]からの阪急バス箕面森町線にのりかえられる。 * JR山陰線[[亀岡駅]]から京阪京都交通・亀岡市ふるさとバスのりかえ「神地」停留所（亀岡市西別院町、国道423号線沿い）から徒歩5.4km。 * 能勢電鉄妙見線「[[妙見口駅|妙見口]]」駅（豊能町吉川）から徒歩3.6km（新滝道コース経由の場合）。能勢電鉄では「大堂越コース」「新滝道コース」「上杉尾根コース」「初谷渓谷コース」「天台山コース」の5ルートをハイキングコースとして紹介している。{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=のせでんハイキング　妙見山ハイキングの定番コース紹介 |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=能勢電鉄株式会社}} ** 冬季以外は[[妙見ケーブル]]や[[妙見の森リフト]]でアクセス可能であったが、[[2023年]][[12月3日]]の運行をもっていずれも廃止された{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=妙見山で展開する「妙見の森関連事業」の営業終了の繰上げおよび鋼索線（ケーブル）の廃止繰上届の提出について |format=PDF |publisher=能勢電鉄 |date=2023-09-22 |accessdate=2023-11-01}}。 * 北大阪急行線[[千里中央駅]]から阪急バス箕面森町線「箕面森町地区センター」停留所で阪急バス妙見口能勢線（平日のみ）にのりかえ「ケーブル黒川駅」停留所下車、上記のハイキングコースに至る。ただし、妙見口能勢線の運行は平日のみで、2024年4月より能勢町による新しい交通形態へ移行するために路線廃止となる{{Cite web |url=https://www.town.nose.osaka.jp/material/files/group/4/2010_siryou1.pdf |title=令和5年度第2回能勢町地域公共交通会議、資料1（令和6年（2024年）4月実施予定の公共交通再編について） |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=能勢町}}。 * 能勢妙見山駐車場入口には、阪急バス「妙見山上」停留所ならびに京都交通「妙見山」停留所があった。 ** 阪急バスによる運行では、阪急茨木市駅・国鉄茨木駅からの直通便は1967年、内本町二丁目・梅田からの直通便は1967年、阪急池田駅からの直通便は1997年12月に廃止された。阪急池田駅からの直通便廃止からは、日祝日に限り、豊能町余野でバスをのりかえることで移動することができたほか、2011年度までは正月三が日には池田からの臨時直通バスも運行されていた。この余野～妙見山上系統は、2019年7月13日のダイヤ改正で廃止された（最終運行日は2019年7月7日）。 ** 京都交通による運行では、京都駅前（かつては京都祇園）・阪急桂駅・JR亀岡駅からの直通便があったが2005年4月に廃止された。 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[日本の山一覧]] * [[ふるさと兵庫50山]] == 外部リンク == * [https://bunamori.org/ 能勢妙見山ブナ守の会] * [http://www.myoken.org/ 能勢妙見山公式HP] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ 能勢妙見山　妙見宗総本山　本瀧寺] * [http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/cgi-bin/watchizu.cgi?id=52353305&slidex=900&slidey=1200 国土地理院 地図閲覧システム 2万5千分1地形図名：妙見山 (南東)] {{Commons|Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} {{北摂山系の交通、名所}} {{関西百名山}} {{大阪50山}} {{大阪みどりの百選}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みようけんさん おおさかふひようこ}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:丹波高地の山]] [[Category:兵庫県の山]] [[Category:大阪府の山]] [[Category:兵庫県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:大阪府の自然景勝地]] [[Category:川西市の地理]] [[Category:豊能町の地理]] [[Category:能勢町の地理]] [[Category:県境]]

revision.comment: Bot: Creating redirect for [[Template:大阪みどりの百選]] triggered link.
revision.text: #redirect[[:en:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市|12=draft}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡|12=draft}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyogo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}} and {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=draft}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|12=draft}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of {{ill|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)|ja|真如寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nose, Osaka|ja|能勢町|12=draft}}, and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class {{ill|Triangulation station|ja|三角点|12=draft}} is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for {{ill|Japanese beech|ja|ブナ|12=draft}} at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|川西市|12=draft}}, and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and {{ill|Izumikatsuragi Mountain|ja|和泉葛城山|12=draft}} are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|12=draft}}, descended from {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|ja|源満仲|12=draft}}, began venerating {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|12=draft}}. In the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, Nissei, the 21st abbot of {{ill|Kuon-ji|ja|久遠寺|12=draft}}, carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|12=draft}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|12=draft}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|12=draft}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|12=draft}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|12=draft}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|12=draft}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=draft}}. * From {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|12=draft}} (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From {{ill|Kameoka Station|ja|亀岡駅|12=draft}} (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From {{ill|Myōken-guchi Station|ja|妙見口駅|12=draft}} (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} {{Hokusetsu Mountains Transport and Attractions}} {{Kansai 100 Famous Mountains}} {{Osaka 50 Mountains}} {{Osaka Green 100 Selection}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Tamba Highlands]] [[Category:Mountains of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Hyōgo]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Osaka]] [[Category:Kawanishi, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Toyono, Osaka]] [[Category:Nose, Osaka]] [[Category:Prefectural Borders]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[ja:妙見山_(大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[en:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)}}

Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} {{Hokusetsu Mountains Transport and Attractions}} {{Kansai 100 Famous Mountains}} {{Osaka 50 Mountains}} {{Osaka Green 100 Selection}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Tamba Highlands]] [[Category:Mountains of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Hyōgo]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Osaka]] [[Category:Kawanishi, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Toyono, Osaka]] [[Category:Nose, Osaka]] [[Category:Prefectural Borders]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[ja:妙見山_(大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[en:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)}}

The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for {{ill|Japanese beech|ja|ブナ|12=draft}} at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|川西市|12=draft}}, and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and {{ill|Izumikatsuragi Mountain|ja|和泉葛城山|12=draft}} are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|12=draft}}, descended from {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|ja|源満仲|12=draft}}, began venerating {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|12=draft}}. In the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, Nissei, the 21st abbot of {{ill|Kuon-ji|ja|久遠寺|12=draft}}, carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|12=draft}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|12=draft}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|12=draft}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|12=draft}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|12=draft}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|12=draft}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=draft}}. * From {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|12=draft}} (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From {{ill|Kameoka Station|ja|亀岡駅|12=draft}} (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From {{ill|Myōken-guchi Station|ja|妙見口駅|12=draft}} (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} {{Hokusetsu Mountains Transport and Attractions}} {{Kansai 100 Famous Mountains}} {{Osaka 50 Mountains}} {{Osaka Green 100 Selection}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Tamba Highlands]] [[Category:Mountains of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Hyōgo]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Osaka]] [[Category:Kawanishi, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Toyono, Osaka]] [[Category:Nose, Osaka]] [[Category:Prefectural Borders]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[ja:妙見山_(大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[en:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)}}

{{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市|12=draft}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡|12=draft}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyogo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}} and {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=draft}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|12=draft}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of {{ill|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)|ja|真如寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nose, Osaka|ja|能勢町|12=draft}}, and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class {{ill|Triangulation station|ja|三角点|12=draft}} is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|川西市|12=draft}}, and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and {{ill|Izumikatsuragi Mountain|ja|和泉葛城山|12=draft}} are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|12=draft}}, descended from {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|ja|源満仲|12=draft}}, began venerating {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|12=draft}}. In the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, Nissei, the 21st abbot of {{ill|Kuon-ji|ja|久遠寺|12=draft}}, carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|12=draft}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|12=draft}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|12=draft}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|12=draft}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|12=draft}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|12=draft}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=draft}}. * From {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|12=draft}} (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From {{ill|Kameoka Station|ja|亀岡駅|12=draft}} (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From {{ill|Myōken-guchi Station|ja|妙見口駅|12=draft}} (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} {{Hokusetsu Mountains Transport and Attractions}} {{Kansai 100 Famous Mountains}} {{Osaka 50 Mountains}} {{Osaka Green 100 Selection}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Tamba Highlands]] [[Category:Mountains of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Hyōgo]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Osaka]] [[Category:Kawanishi, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Toyono, Osaka]] [[Category:Nose, Osaka]] [[Category:Prefectural Borders]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[ja:妙見山_(大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[en:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)}}

The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)}}. * From {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From {{ill|Kameoka Station|ja|亀岡駅}} (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From {{ill|Myōken-guchi Station|ja|妙見口駅}} (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} {{Hokusetsu Mountains Transport and Attractions}} {{Kansai 100 Famous Mountains}} {{Osaka 50 Mountains}} {{Osaka Green 100 Selection}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Tamba Highlands]] [[Category:Mountains of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Hyōgo]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Osaka]] [[Category:Kawanishi, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Toyono, Osaka]] [[Category:Nose, Osaka]] [[Category:Prefectural Borders]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[sn:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[ja:妙見山_(大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[en:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)}}

{{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyogo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} and {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of {{ill|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)|ja|真如寺}} in {{ill|Nose, Osaka|ja|能勢町}}, and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class {{ill|Triangulation station|ja|三角点}} is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for {{ill|Japanese beech|ja|ブナ}} at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|川西市}}, and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and {{ill|Izumikatsuragi Mountain|ja|和泉葛城山}} are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏}}, descended from {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|ja|源満仲}}, began venerating {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}. In the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, Nissei, the 21st abbot of {{ill|Kuon-ji|ja|久遠寺}}, carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)}}. * From {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From {{ill|Kameoka Station|ja|亀岡駅}} (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From {{ill|Myōken-guchi Station|ja|妙見口駅}} (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} {{Hokusetsu Mountains Transport and Attractions}} {{Kansai 100 Famous Mountains}} {{Osaka 50 Mountains}} {{Osaka Green 100 Selection}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Tamba Highlands]] [[Category:Mountains of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Hyōgo]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Osaka]] [[Category:Kawanishi, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Toyono, Osaka]] [[Category:Nose, Osaka]] [[Category:Prefectural Borders]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[sn:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[ja:妙見山_(大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[en:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)}}

Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]] {{Hokusetsu Mountains Transport and Attractions}} {{Kansai 100 Famous Mountains}} {{Osaka 50 Mountains}} {{Osaka Green 100 Selection}} }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)}} {{translated page|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|version=104364261|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[sn:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[ja:妙見山_(大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[en:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]]

{{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市|12=draft}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡|12=draft}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyogo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}} and {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=draft}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|12=draft}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of {{ill|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)|ja|真如寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nose, Osaka|ja|能勢町|12=draft}}, and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class {{ill|Triangulation station|ja|三角点|12=draft}} is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for {{ill|Japanese beech|ja|ブナ|12=draft}} at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|川西市|12=draft}}, and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and {{ill|Izumikatsuragi Mountain|ja|和泉葛城山|12=draft}} are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|12=draft}}, descended from {{ill|Minamoto no Mitsunaka|ja|源満仲|12=draft}}, began venerating {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|12=draft}}. In the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, Nissei, the 21st abbot of {{ill|Kuon-ji|ja|久遠寺|12=draft}}, carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|12=draft}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|12=draft}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|12=draft}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|12=draft}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|12=draft}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|12=draft}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=draft}}. * From {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|12=draft}} (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From {{ill|Kameoka Station|ja|亀岡駅|12=draft}} (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From {{ill|Myōken-guchi Station|ja|妙見口駅|12=draft}} (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]] {{Hokusetsu Mountains Transport and Attractions}} {{Kansai 100 Famous Mountains}} {{Osaka 50 Mountains}} {{Osaka Green 100 Selection}} }} {{translated page|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|version=104364261|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] }} [[sn:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[ja:妙見山_(大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[en:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)}}
