During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose clan|qq=}}, descended from [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], began venerating [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. In the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Nissei, the 21st abbot of [[:en:Kuon-ji|Kuon-ji]], carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|qq=}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken sect|qq=}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirataki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白滝神社}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Falls|qq=}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From [[:en:Kameoka Station|Kameoka Station]] (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From [[:en:Myōkenguchi Station|Myōken-guchi Station]] (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of mountains in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]] {{Hokusetsu Mountains Transport and Attractions}} {{Kansai 100 Famous Mountains}} {{Osaka 50 Mountains}} {{Osaka Green 100 Selection}} }} {{translated page|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|version=104364261|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] }} [[sn:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[ja:妙見山_(大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[Category:Mountains of Tanba Highlands]] [[Category:Mountains of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic Sites of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural scenic spots of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Geography of Kawanishi City]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] [[Category:Geography of Nose Town]] [[Category:豊能町の地理]] [[ceb:Myōken San (bukid sa Hapon, lat 34,93, long 135,47)]] {{Commons category|Mount Myoken (Nose)}} [[ro:Myōken]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:豊能町の地理]] into [[Category:Geography of Toyono Town]] via bot
revision.text:
{{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi, Hyōgo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyono District, Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyōgo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] and [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of [[:en:Zhenru Temple|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)]] in [[:en:Nose, Osaka|Nose, Osaka]], and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class [[:en:Triangulation station|Triangulation station]] is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for [[:en:Fagus crenata|Japanese beech]] at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]], and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and [[:en:Mount Izumi Katsuragi|Izumikatsuragi Mountain]] are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose clan|qq=}}, descended from [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], began venerating [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. In the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Nissei, the 21st abbot of [[:en:Kuon-ji|Kuon-ji]], carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|qq=}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken sect|qq=}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirataki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白滝神社}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Falls|qq=}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From [[:en:Kameoka Station|Kameoka Station]] (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From [[:en:Myōkenguchi Station|Myōken-guchi Station]] (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of mountains in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] {{Commons|Category:original pages]] {{Hokusetsu Mountains Transport and Attractions}} {{Kansai 100 Famous Mountains}} {{Osaka 50 Mountains}} {{Osaka Green 100 Selection}} }} {{translated page|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|version=104364261|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] }} [[sn:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[ja:妙見山_(大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[Category:Mountains of Tanba Highlands]] [[Category:Mountains of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic Sites of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural scenic spots of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Geography of Kawanishi City]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] [[Category:Geography of Nose Town]] [[Category:Geography of Toyono Town]] [[ceb:Myōken San (bukid sa Hapon, lat 34,93, long 135,47)]] {{Commons category|Mount Myoken (Nose)}} [[ro:Myōken]]

This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of [[:en:Zhenru Temple|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)]] in [[:en:Nose, Osaka|Nose, Osaka]], and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class [[:en:Triangulation station|Triangulation station]] is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for [[:en:Fagus crenata|Japanese beech]] at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]], and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and [[:en:Mount Izumi Katsuragi|Izumikatsuragi Mountain]] are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose clan|qq=|1=Nose clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611932}}, descended from [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], began venerating [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. In the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Nissei, the 21st abbot of [[:en:Kuon-ji|Kuon-ji]], carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|qq=|1=Settsu Meisho Zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498549}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken sect|qq=}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirataki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白滝神社|1=Shirataki-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580051}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Falls|qq=|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From [[:en:Kameoka Station|Kameoka Station]] (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From [[:en:Myōkenguchi Station|Myōken-guchi Station]] (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] (大阪府・兵庫県)|version=104364261|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] }} [[sn:Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)]] [[ja:妙見山_(大阪府・兵庫県)]] [[Category:Mountains of Tanba Highlands]] [[Category:Mountains of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic Sites of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[ro:Myōken]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447024]] Wikidata name: Mount Myōken [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi, Hyōgo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyono District, Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyōgo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] and [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of [[:en:Zhenru Temple|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)]] in [[:en:Nose, Osaka|Nose, Osaka]], and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class [[:en:Triangulation station|Triangulation station]] is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for [[:en:Fagus crenata|Japanese beech]] at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]], and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and [[:en:Mount Izumi Katsuragi|Izumikatsuragi Mountain]] are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose clan|qq=|1=Nose clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611932}}, descended from [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], began venerating [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. In the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Nissei, the 21st abbot of [[:en:Kuon-ji|Kuon-ji]], carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|qq=|1=Settsu Meisho Zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498549}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken sect|qq=}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirataki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白滝神社|1=Shirataki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580051}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Falls|qq=|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From [[:en:Kameoka Station|Kameoka Station]] (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From [[:en:Myōkenguchi Station|Myōken-guchi Station]] (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of mountains in Japan|qq=|1=list of mountains and hills of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505923}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|qq=|1=50 Hyōgo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277298}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] (大阪府・兵庫県)|version=104364261|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] }} In the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Nissei, the 21st abbot of [[:en:Kuon-ji|Kuon-ji]], carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|qq=|1=Settsu Meisho Zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498549}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken sect|qq=}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirataki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白滝神社|1=Shirataki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580051}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Falls|qq=|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From [[:en:Kameoka Station|Kameoka Station]] (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From [[:en:Myōkenguchi Station|Myōken-guchi Station]] (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in [[ceb:Myōken San (bukid sa Hapon, lat 34,93, long 135,47)]][[commons:Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)]][[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]][[ro:Myōken]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447024]] Wikidata name: Mount Myōken [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi, Hyōgo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyono District, Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyōgo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] and [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of [[:en:Zhenru Temple|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)]] in [[:en:Nose, Osaka|Nose, Osaka]], and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class [[:en:Triangulation station|Triangulation station]] is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for [[:en:Fagus crenata|Japanese beech]] at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]], and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and [[:en:Mount Izumi Katsuragi|Izumikatsuragi Mountain]] are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. In the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Nissei, the 21st abbot of [[:en:Kuon-ji|Kuon-ji]], carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|qq=|1=Settsu Meisho Zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498549}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken sect|qq=}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirataki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白滝神社|1=Shirataki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580051}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Falls|qq=|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From [[:en:Kameoka Station|Kameoka Station]] (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From [[:en:Myōkenguchi Station|Myōken-guchi Station]] (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Town]] [[Category:Geography of Toyono Town]] [[ceb:Myōken San (bukid sa Hapon, lat 34,93, long 135,47)]] {{Commons category|Mount Myoken (Nose)}} [[ro:Myōken]] {{wikidata link|Q11447024}}

[[ceb:Myōken San (bukid sa Hapon, lat 34,93, long 135,47)]][[sn:commons:Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)]][[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]][[ro:Myōken]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447024]] Wikidata name: Mount Myōken [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi, Hyōgo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyono District, Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyōgo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] and [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of [[:en:Zhenru Temple|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)]] in [[:en:Nose, Osaka|Nose, Osaka]], and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class [[:en:Triangulation station|Triangulation station]] is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for [[:en:Fagus crenata|Japanese beech]] at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]], and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and [[:en:Mount Izumi Katsuragi|Izumikatsuragi Mountain]] are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose clan|qq=|1=Nose clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611932}}, descended from [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], began venerating [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. In the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Nissei, the 21st abbot of [[:en:Kuon-ji|Kuon-ji]], carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|qq=|1=Settsu Meisho Zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498549}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken sect|qq=}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirataki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白滝神社|1=Shirataki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580051}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Falls|qq=|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From [[:en:Kameoka Station|Kameoka Station]] (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From [[:en:Myōkenguchi Station|Myōken-guchi Station]] (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of mountains in Japan|qq=|1=list of mountains and hills of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505923}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|qq=|1=50 Hyōgo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277298}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|1=Settsu Meisho Zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498549}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken sect|qq=}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirataki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白滝神社|1=Shirataki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580051}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Falls|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From [[:en:Kameoka Station|Kameoka Station]] (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From [[:en:Myōkenguchi Station|Myōken-guchi Station]] (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of mountains in Japan|1=list of mountains and hills of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505923}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|1=50 Hyōgo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277298}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] revision.text:
[[ceb:Myōken San (bukid sa Hapon, lat 34,93, long 135,47)]][[sn:commons:Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)]][[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]][[ro:Myōken]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447024]] Wikidata name: Mount Myōken [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyono District, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyōgo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] and [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of [[:en:Zhenru Temple|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)]] in [[:en:Nose, Osaka|Nose, Osaka]], and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class [[:en:Triangulation station|Triangulation station]] is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for [[:en:Fagus crenata|Japanese beech]] at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]], and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and [[:en:Mount Izumi Katsuragi|Izumikatsuragi Mountain]] are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose clan|1=Nose clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611932}}, descended from [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], began venerating [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. In the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Nissei, the 21st abbot of [[:en:Kuon-ji|Kuon-ji]], carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|1=Settsu Meisho Zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498549}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken sect|qq=}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirataki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白滝神社|1=Shirataki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580051}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Falls|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From [[:en:Kameoka Station|Kameoka Station]] (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From [[:en:Myōkenguchi Station|Myōken-guchi Station]] (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of mountains in Japan|1=list of mountains and hills of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505923}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|1=50 Hyōgo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277298}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] [[ceb:Myōken San (bukid sa Hapon, lat 34,93, long 135,47)]][[sn:commons:Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)]][[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]][[ro:Myōken]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447024]] Wikidata name: Mount Myōken [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyono District, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyōgo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] and [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of [[:en:Zhenru Temple|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)]] in [[:en:Nose, Osaka|Nose, Osaka]], and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class [[:en:Triangulation station|Triangulation station]] is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for [[:en:Fagus crenata|Japanese beech]] at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]], and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and [[:en:Mount Izumi Katsuragi|Izumikatsuragi Mountain]] are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose clan|1=Nose clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611932}}, descended from [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], began venerating [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. In the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Nissei, the 21st abbot of [[:en:Kuon-ji|Kuon-ji]], carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|1=Settsu Meisho Zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498549}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken sect|qq=}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirataki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白滝神社|1=Shirataki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580051}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Falls|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From [[:en:Kameoka Station|Kameoka Station]] (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From [[:en:Myōkenguchi Station|Myōken-guchi Station]] (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of mountains in Japan|1=list of mountains and hills of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505923}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|1=50 Hyōgo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277298}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirataki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白滝神社|1=Shirataki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580051}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Falls|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From [[:en:Kameoka Station|Kameoka Station]] (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From [[:en:Myōkenguchi Station|Myōken-guchi Station]] (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of mountains in Japan|1=list of mountains and hills of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505923}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|1=50 Hyōgo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277298}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] [[ceb:Myōken San (bukid sa Hapon, lat 34,93, long 135,47)]][[sn:commons:Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)]][[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]][[ro:Myōken]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447024]] Wikidata name: Mount Myōken [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyono District, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyōgo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] and [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of [[:en:Zhenru Temple|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)]] in [[:en:Nose, Osaka|Nose, Osaka]], and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class [[:en:Triangulation station|Triangulation station]] is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for [[:en:Fagus crenata|Japanese beech]] at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]], and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and [[:en:Mount Izumi Katsuragi|Izumikatsuragi Mountain]] are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose clan|1=Nose clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611932}}, descended from [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], began venerating [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. In the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Nissei, the 21st abbot of [[:en:Kuon-ji|Kuon-ji]], carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|1=Settsu Meisho Zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498549}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken sect|qq=}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirataki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白滝神社|1=Shirataki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580051}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Falls|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From [[:en:Kameoka Station|Kameoka Station]] (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From [[:en:Myōkenguchi Station|Myōken-guchi Station]] (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of mountains in Japan|1=list of mountains and hills of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505923}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|1=50 Hyōgo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277298}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose clan|1=Nose clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611932}}, descended from [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], began venerating [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. [[ceb:Myōken San (bukid sa Hapon, lat 34,93, long 135,47)]][[commons:Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)]][[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]][[ro:Myōken]] [[ceb:Myōken San (bukid sa Hapon, lat 34,93, long 135,47)]][[sn:commons:Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)]][[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]][[ro:Myōken]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11447024]] Wikidata name: Mount Myōken [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Nose Myokenzan|sacred site near the summit|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|temple midway up|Hontaki-ji}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Myoken | image = [[File:Mount Myoken (Nose)1.jpg|240px]] | Elevation_m = 660.1 | Range = [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|55|44.328|N|135|27|59.245|E|display=inline}} | Location = {{ill|Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県川西市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi, Hyōgo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},
{{ill|Toyono District, Osaka|ja|大阪府豊能郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyono District, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Toyono Town and Nose Town) | Map = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan Hyōgo Prefecture | Relief = 1 | Map_caption = Location of Mount Myoken in Osaka and Hyōgo | OSM_zoom = 10 }} '''Mount Myoken''' (妙見山) is a mountain straddling [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] and [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], part of the [[Hokusetsu Mountain Range]]. == Overview == The mountain itself is a subject of worship. Near the summit is ''{{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', an important {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple in the Kansai region. This temple, officially named Murosan Shinnyo-ji Keigai Butsudō Nose Myokenzan, is an enclave of [[:en:Zhenru Temple|Shinnyo-ji (Osaka Prefecture)]] in [[:en:Nose, Osaka|Nose, Osaka]], and attracts far more visitors than its parent temple. A fourth-class [[:en:Triangulation station|Triangulation station]] is located at the summit, within Toyono Town, Osaka. The triangulation marker is discreetly placed behind a memorial stone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.google.com/maps/@34.9288684,135.4673572,3a,90y,273.91h,30.3t/data=!3m8!1e1!3m6!1sAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg!2e10!3e11!6shttps:%2F%2Flh5.googleusercontent.com%2Fp%2FAF1QipM6B_3w0CwvMR8Wu_YVqLdcIHiS5HpEXgePnAeg%3Dw203-h100-k-no-pi-0-ya76.70306-ro-0-fo100!7i8704!8i4352?hl=ja |title=Google Street View: Mount Myoken Triangulation Point |access-date=2017-12-17 |date=2016-05 |publisher=[[Google Street View]] }}. Mount Myoken marks the southernmost natural habitat for [[:en:Fagus crenata|Japanese beech]] at altitudes of around 600 meters in Southwest Japan. Previously thought confined to Osaka Prefecture, eight beech trees have been identified in [[:en:Kawanishi, Hyōgo|Kawanishi, Hyōgo]], and a portion of the 9.5-hectare beech forest near the summit has been confirmed to extend into Hyōgo.http://www.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/JPN/apr/hyogoshizen/reddata2010/rdb2010/data/tan-itsu/tan-itsu_H25_10.pdfhttp://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/hanshin/201503/0007829215.shtmlhttp://bunamori.org/ The beech habitats in Mount Myoken and [[:en:Mount Izumi Katsuragi|Izumikatsuragi Mountain]] are designated as Natural Monuments. == History == === Summit === The summit was once home to Foraku-san Daikū-ji, reportedly founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the {{ill|Nose clan|ja|能勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose clan|1=Nose clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611932}}, descended from [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], began venerating [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. In the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Nissei, the 21st abbot of [[:en:Kuon-ji|Kuon-ji]], carved a new image of Myōken under the patronage of Lord Nose Yoritsugu and enshrined it in a temple built at Daikū-ji's former site, now known as Nose Myokenzan. The summit’s beech forest, over 10,000 years old, suggests that the site was a place of worship long before Daikū-ji's establishment. === Middle Slopes and Waterfalls === On the northern slope lies {{ill|Hontaki|ja|本滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, historically a site for ascetic practices since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The falls are described in the {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|1=Settsu Meisho Zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498549}} as “Myoken Falls,” frequented by Nose Myokenzan worshippers. Adjacent to the falls was Jōtomi-dō, later rebuilt as {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}} in 1935 by Nichiren priest Noma Nisshō, becoming the head temple of {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myoken sect|ja|妙見宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken sect|qq=}}. Many stone statues along the path to the falls reflect its past as a religious training ground. Other waterfalls include {{ill|Shintaki|ja|新滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (southwest slope) near {{ill|Shirataki Shrine|ja|白瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirataki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白滝神社|1=Shirataki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580051}} and {{ill|Seiryū Falls|ja|清滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Falls|1=Seiryū Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561300}} (east slope) near Myōryū-ji Temple. == Access == For detailed information on access to the summit (Nose Myokenzan Main Hall), see {{ill|Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja|能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Myokenzan (Nichiren Buddhism)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] (Hankyu Takarazuka Line), take the Hankyu Bus to Myōken-guchi (4 km hike). * From [[:en:Kameoka Station|Kameoka Station]] (JR Sagano Line), take the Keihan Kyoto Kotsu bus to Jinchi (5.4 km hike). * From [[:en:Myōkenguchi Station|Myōken-guchi Station]] (Nose Electric Railway), hike 3.6 km via the Shintaki Trail. Five hiking routes are available.{{Cite web |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/hiking/course.html |title=Hiking Routes for Mount Myoken |access-date=2023-11-29 |publisher=Nose Electric Railway }} * Cable cars and chairlifts ceased operation on December 3, 2023.{{cite pressrelease |url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/upload_file/noseden/information/newsrelease202309221.pdf |title=End of Myoken Cable and Related Services |format=PDF |publisher=Nose Electric Railway |date=2023-09-22 |access-date=2023-11-01}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of mountains in Japan|ja|日本の山一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of mountains in Japan|1=list of mountains and hills of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505923}} * {{ill|Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|ja|ふるさと兵庫50山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furusato Hyōgo 50 Mountains|1=50 Hyōgo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11277298}} == External Links == * [https://bunamori.org/ Nose Myokenzan Beech Conservation Society] * [http://www.myoken.org/ Official Website of Nose Myokenzan] * [http://www.hontakiji.com/ Hontaki-ji, Myoken-sect Head Temple] (大阪府・兵庫県)|version=104364261|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mount Myoken (Nose)}} [[ja:妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)]] }}
